per_review.translated_text: not everyone plays on expensive screens most people have 1080p screens so why go for more powerful cards when a 1080p card is cheaper, if i have a 1080p screen im not putting in extra hundreds of dollars for a 1440p or 4k card with settings i dont use

per_review.original:
I have the 4070 version, and i benchmarked rainbow six siege
At 1080p I'm getting 350 fps
On 1440p I'm getting 240 fps
So before buying the 4090 version, think to yourself, is going from 350 fps to 360 fps worth paying double the price for the same laptop?
per_review.translated_text: I have the 4070 version, and I benchmarked Rainbow Six Siege. At 1080p I'm getting 350 fps. On 1440p I'm getting 240 fps. So before buying the 4090 version, think to yourself, is going from 350 fps to 360 fps worth paying double the price for the same laptop?

Prediction: Going from 350 fps to 360 fps not worth it

per_review.original:
This garbage laptop!
I was getting 20-30 watt load on my 13900H CPU in the M16 (2023).
Today, I removed the liquid metal. Half the CPU die wasn't covered by the liquid metal.
I went from 20 watts due to thermal throttling while gaming to easily 40 to 90 watts after replacing the thermal grease.
per_review.translated_text: This garbage laptop! I was getting 20-30 watt load on my 13900H CPU in the M16 (2023). Today, I removed the liquid metal. Half the CPU die wasn't covered by the liquid metal. I went from 20 watts due to thermal throttling while gaming to easily 40 to 90 watts after replacing the thermal grease.

Praise: Easily 40 to 90 watts after replacing thermal grease
Suggestion: Cover the entire cpu die with liquid metal

sub_issues.name: 1080p vs Higher Resolutions
sample_quotes: not everyone plays on expensive screens most people have 1080p screens so why go for more powerful cards when a 1080p card is cheaper, if i have a 1080p screen im not putting in extra hundreds of dollars for a 1440p or 4k card with settings i dont use

I have the 4070 version, and I benchmarked Rainbow Six Siege. At 1080p I'm getting 350 fps. On 1440p I'm getting 240 fps. So before buying the 4090 version, think to yourself, is going from 350 fps to 360 fps worth paying double the price for the same laptop?
I figure I probably won't ever bother going higher than 1440p on a monitor-sized display. Just no point. I'd rather marvel at my 600fps or something in CS on these super powerful specs.
sub_issues.narrative: The focus on 1080p vs higher resolutions stems from the fact that many gamers still use 1080p monitors, making high-end cards like the RTX 4090 seem unnecessary for their needs. This primarily affects budget-conscious gamers who prioritize cost over performance, as they may not see the value in investing in a powerful GPU when their current setup suffices.

sub_issues.name: 4K and 8K Visual Differences
sample_quotes: I have sat down in front 8k gaming setup and 4k gaming setup for CP2077. Editors (Google it) alike say visually its huge difference. Once your faced with 8k assets on a 8k screen. 4K just looks broken. Yes lower prices and bring on 8k gaming. ❤

And in all of the reviews I've seen they are saying this really makes the 4k content noticeably clearer compares to 4k content on 4k TVs, it's more like 5k or 6k, even 7k-ish depending on the type of the actual content.

Highest end VR headsets are pushing two 4K screens right now, once this kind of hardware becomes more financially accessible I think we will be moving further in the direction of higher resolution VR.
sub_issues.narrative: The high ratings for 4K and 8K visual differences indicate that users who experience these resolutions appreciate the significant enhancements in clarity and detail. This is particularly relevant for enthusiasts and professionals who invest in high-end setups, as they are more likely to notice and benefit from the improvements offered by the RTX 4090.

_analysis_confidence.label: MODERATE
_analysis_confidence.statement: Based on 34 reviews from 1 platform. Strongest signal: Questioning value of high-end cards (0.67). Weakest: Display issues after replacement (0.54, only 2 reviews).

cross_insights.description: "Questioning value of high-end cards" dominates at 76% of all reviews. This is the primary signal — other themes are secondary.
Strong consensus: 100% of the 4 reviewers who mention "performance" are negative. This is a confirmed weakness.
Strong consensus: 100% of the 3 reviewers who mention "design" are negative. This is a confirmed weakness.

consumer.headline: Questioning Value of High-End Cards
consumer.summary:
The RTX 4090 faces skepticism with a TrustScore of 44.0 and 0% recommend rate. Buyers criticize its performance and design, with 100% negative feedback in both categories. Those seeking high-end graphics may want to reconsider.

⚠ Quality note: Clarify that the primary concern is performance and design issues, with price being secondary.
consumer.recommendation: Wait for more reviews before considering a purchase.

company.headline: Urgent Need to Address Performance and Design Issues
company.summary:
The RTX 4090 is struggling significantly with performance and design, reflected in a 44.0 TrustScore. With 100% negative reviews on both aspects, immediate improvements are crucial to regain buyer confidence. The price point is also a major concern, with 71% of mentions being negative.

⚠ Quality note: Clarify that the primary concern is performance and design issues, with price being secondary.
key_takeaways: 100% negative feedback on performance and design.
Price is a significant pain point for buyers.
Only 2.9% of buyers intend to purchase.
Immediate action needed to improve product perception.
company.strategic_priority: Focus on enhancing performance and design in the next quarter.
company.marketing_lead: Highlight any improvements in performance and design in future campaigns.

_quality_flags: Quality note: Clarify that the primary concern is performance and design issues, with price being secondary.
The analysis confidence is moderate, which may not justify strong assertions.

_self_correction.quality_note: The analysis has some relevant insights but contains off-topic content.

purchase_advice.one_line: Consider waiting for better value or price adjustments before purchasing the RTX 4090.

personas.name: Gamer
personas.reason: While the performance is praised, the overwhelming concern about the value of high-end cards suggests waiting for a price drop or better alternatives.

personas.name: Professional
personas.reason: The performance is good for demanding tasks, but the concerns about driver compatibility and value indicate it may be wise to hold off.

personas.name: Casual
personas.reason: The high price and mixed reviews on value make it unsuitable for casual users who may not need top-tier performance.

alternatives.why: Offers competitive performance at a potentially lower price point.
alternatives.when_better: If you prioritize value and performance without the highest price tag.
alternatives.when_worse: If you specifically need features exclusive to NVIDIA cards.

alternatives.why: A more affordable option with solid performance for most gaming and professional tasks.
alternatives.when_better: If you want high performance without the extreme cost of the 4090.
alternatives.when_worse: If you require the absolute best performance available.

Can i have pc that you dont need please bro i watch your videos plus i fixed my friends pc with your tips

This garbage laptop!
I was getting 20-30 watt load on my 13900H CPU in the M16 (2023).
Today, I removed the liquid metal. Half the CPU die wasn't covered by the liquid metal.
I went from 20 watts due to thermal throttling while gaming to easily 40 to 90 watts after replacing the thermal grease.

action_items.item: Evaluate pricing strategy to enhance perceived value for high-end graphics cards.
Consider bundling with software or games to increase value proposition.
Highlight unique features that differentiate from mid-range options.
Investigate display panel quality and compatibility for replacements.
Enhance quality control for display components.
Provide clearer guidelines for display replacement procedures.
Enhance driver support for Windows to match Linux ease of installation.
Streamline driver installation process for Windows users.
Regularly update drivers to ensure compatibility with the latest games.
Provide clear pricing information on the product page.
Include a price comparison with competitors.
Offer financing options for potential buyers.
